title: centaurea malatyensis
intro: Centaurea malatyensis, also known as Malatya knapweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Turkey, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Centaurea malatyensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Centaurea malatyensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in herbal baths and topical applications for skin conditions and wound healing.
